item.title: Dbrand Claims Its New Joy-Con Holder Is More Comfortable Than A Pro Controller
item.content:encoded:
Well, we appreciate the enthusiasm

Accessory manufacturer dbrand has launched a new controller holder for Switch 2 that it claims is more comfortable than a Pro Controller.

It's essentially a plastic case in which to fit your Joy-Con 2 controllers, kinda like the 'JoyVerse' grip from JSAUX, except this one seems to have some sort of hinge to allow for some angle adjustment.

"Introducing a new way to hold your Joy-Cons: the Joy-Lock Controller Holder. It's 80% cheaper than a Pro Controller, yet somehow even more comfortable."

On its own, the controller holder is priced at $19.95, but it's also designed to be used in conjunction with dbrand's 'Joy-Lock Grips', which are also priced at $19.95. Granted, if you get them both together, dbrand will knock $5 off to make it $34.90.

Then you've got some Stick Grips for the analogue controllers for an extra $9.95. So if you go for everything, you're looking at $44.95 which, yes, is about half what you'd expect to pay for an official Pro Controller, but is this thing really more comfortable..?

Honestly, we have our doubts.

And that's nothing against dbrand. Even with some solid grips, the Joy-Con are still, well... Joy-Con. They simply don't compare to the Pro Controller. You gotta be bold when you're trying to sell your wares though, so we admire the enthusiasm.

Here's a look at dbrand's new controller holder:

And for comparison, here's the Pro Controller:

To give dbrand some credit, we actually thought its Killswitch Case for the Switch 2 was pretty great, awarding it a score of 8/10 in our review for its design and comfort.

What do you make of dbrand's new controller holder? Reckon its claim holds any weight? Let us know with a comment.

[source dbrand.com]

Nintendo Life’s resident horror fanatic, when he’s not knee-deep in Resident Evil and Silent Hill lore, Ollie likes to dive into a good horror book while nursing a lovely cup of tea. He also enjoys long walks and listens to everything from TOOL to Chuck Berry.

  • Author Profile
  • Bluesky
  • Reply

item.title: PlayStation 5 Linux Breakthrough Lands A Little Late, But It Could Reshape How Some Owners Use Their Consoles
item.content:encoded:
Ever since the system's release in late 2020, developers have been hard at work expanding the PlayStation 5's functionality beyond what Sony intended, helped by leaks that could lead to permanent jailbreaks. Now, over five years since the console's release, those with an older phat system still running older versions of the system software can turn Sony's current generation into a highly capable Linux PC, and take advantage of its Zen 2 8 CPU cores and RDNA 2 GPU to run emulators and Steam games with impressive fluidity thanks to a new loader released online by Andy Nguyen a.k.a. TheFl0w.

"ps5-linux leverages a patched HV vulnerability to transform your PS5 Phat console running 3.xx or 4.xx firmwares into a highly capable Linux PC, unlocking its full hardware potential for desktop use," the loader GitHub description reads. "Powered by 8 CPU cores (16 threads) at 3.5 GHz and a GPU at 2.23 GHz, it provides enough performance to run Steam games and various emulators with impressive fluidity. It supports HDMI 4K60 video and audio output. Furthermore, it allows you to utilize an M.2 SSD as a dedicated Linux partition, as well as all USB ports on the console."

As already mentioned, not many users will be able to run Linux on PlayStation 5 due to limitations: only select firmwares are supported, and only version 4.0 supports M.2.

  • 3.00, 3.10, 3.20, 3.21, without M.2 support
  • 4.00, 4.02, 4.03, 4.50, 4.51 with M.2 support

The developer, however, could be adding support for other firmwares, including 1.xx and 2.xx firmwares. Support for 5.xx firmwares could be coming as well, but since Linux would have to run within GameOS VM, it may result in reduced performance and other limitations.

If you do happen to have an older PlayStation 5 that you did not update, want to try running Linux on the system, but don't want to lose the system's regular functionality, you are in luck, as this modification doesn't touch the internal M.2 drive. As this is a soft-mod, however, you will need to run the exploit every time you want to boot into Linux, with no option to put it in standby.

Since this is a very fresh release, we have yet to see what can be achieved on a PlayStation 5 running Linux, but we already saw a while back that it can run select Steam games not only flawlessly, but with the additional features that are normally not available in native PS5 versions of the game. As shown last month by developer Andy Nguyen, it is possible to run Grand Theft Auto V Enhanced on the system, complete with ray tracing, at 60 FPS, which is the maximum FPS currently supported by the exploit.

Seeing Linux running on a PlayStation 5 is exciting enough for the possibilities it opens, but it's not exactly new for Sony systems. Those who have been playing video games for some time and were around for the PlayStation 3 launch will surely remember OtherOS, an early feature that allowed users to run Linux on the system. This feature was controversially removed by firmware update 3.21 in April, leading to a class action lawsuit against Sony, which was later dismissed in 2011.

About the author: Francesco De Meo has been covering video games and technology since 2012, starting his career at small outlets like Gamersyndrome and GeekSnack. After joining Wccftech gaming section in 2015, he quickly expanded his video gaming coverage with in-depth reporting, interviews with iconic industry figures such as Grasshopper Manufacture founder and No More Heroes creator Goichi "Suda51" Suda, Resident Evil series creator Shinji Mikami, Team NINJA's president and Nioh series director Fumihiko Yasuda, and Silent Hill creator Keiichiro Toyama, reviews and on-the-ground coverage of major industry events such as Gamescom and E3. When he's not reporting or reviewing, Francesco can be found playing the genres he loves most, spending time with his six cats, reading, writing music, playing guitar and drumming for his progressive rock band.

Follow Wccftech on Google to get more of our news coverage in your feeds.



https://wccftech.com/playstation-5-linux-breakthrough-reshape-consoles/

item.title: American Truck Simulator cruises into Illinois with a fresh expansion releasing mid-May, as SCS also tease a new truck
item.content:encoded:
Your cargo's Chicago-bound

Fresh off adding a new feature that'll make my reckless driving a bit easier to recover from, SCS Software have announced a release date for American Truck Simulator's next expansion, as the game continues its trek from US west coast to East. Illinois is the latest destination and you'll be able to take to its highways on May 14th.

"Illinois showcases the best of what the USA has to offer. Hop into your rig, hit the legendary Route 66, and journey through Springfield, the state’s historic capital, to Chicago, one of the Midwest's most iconic cities," SCS wrote in the DLC's description.

"Travel alongside the mighty Mississippi River, which defines the state’s western border. Discover charming river towns, classic steamboats, and scenic vineyards dotting the landscape. In the southern part of Illinois, experience a blend of cities, lush farmland, and quaint small towns. Meanwhile, the northeastern region is a bustling hub of urban and suburban life, anchored by Chicago, the nation’s third-largest city, nestled on the bank of Lake Michigan."

They add that once Illinois is out, you'll be able to drive the entirety of Route 66 in ATS, with the iconic highway running from the game's initial stomping ground of California all the way through Arizona, New Mexico, Texas, Oklahoma, Kansas, and Missouri before reaching Chicago. I can certainly see myself making a point of driving the length of it once the Road Trip mode SCS announced a while back brings lets me drive a regular car across its version of America.

That said, SCS are clearly still prioritising truckers at the moment, with this DLC date reveal having come alongside a teaser for a new truck set to arrive in the sim at some point soon. The latest version of Volvo's VNL rig looks to be the lorry in question, which is cool, though I do wish ATS had a few more retro-style lorries outside of the likes of the International 9900i and the Kenworth W900 if you opt to deck the latter out in such fashion.

Either way, it'll be interesting to see how big Chicago feels rendered in ATS' scale, as well as breeze through some more midwestern farmland when I fancy a break from runs through the pacific northwestern wilderness.

You can wishlist American Truck Simulator's Illinois expansion on Steam.



https://www.rockpapershotgun.com/american-truck-simulator-cruises-into-illinois-with-a-fresh-expansion-releasing-mid-may-as-scs-also-tease-a-new-truck

item.title: Xbox delivers record monthly active users, but hardware revenue is still in decline
item.content:encoded:
Chris Kerr,

Senior Editor, News,GameDeveloper.com

April 30, 2026

2 Min Read

Xbox hardware revenue remains in decline, but Microsoft said it set new records for monthly active users (MAUs) on Xbox during Q3 FY26.

As noted in the company's latest fiscal report, Gaming revenue decreased by $380 million year-on-year—representing a 7 percent decline over that period. That decrease was driven by tumbling Xbox content and services and Xbox hardware revenue. 

In a supplemental SEC filing, Microsoft noted that Xbox content and services revenue decreased by 5 percent year-on-year, while Xbox hardware revenue fell by 33 percent over the same period driven by "lower volume of consoles sold." 

Xbox hardware revenue has been in steady decline for some time, but Microsoft is now looking to reposition and revitalise its video game division under new Xbox CEO Asha Sharma. 

In a memo sent to global Xbox employees earlier this month, Sharma conceded that Xbox is currently a "challenger" as opposed to a market leader. 

Related:Landfall and Aggro Crab team up for Make-a-Wish game bundle

During a Q3 earnings call, Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella said the company is currently doing the "foundational work required to win back fans and strengthen engagement" across key businesses, including Xbox. 

"In the near term, we are focused on fundamentals, prioritizing quality and serving our core users better," he said. "[...] And you also see this in Xbox where the team is recommitting to our core fans and players, and shaping the future of play. Last week’s Game Pass changes are one example of how we are staying responsive to customer feedback."

Nadella was referencing the recent decision to lower the price of some Xbox Game Pass tiers. That move came just months after Microsoft chose to increase the cost of the subscription service. 

Sharma recently told Xbox workers that daily active players will be the division's "north star" moving forward. It appears the company is already making progress on that front.

"We set new records for monthly Xbox active users in the quarter, as well as game streaming hours," said Microsoft CFO Amy Hood during the earnings call. 

Looking ahead to Q4, Hood said Xbox revenue is expected to remain in decline across the board. 

"In Xbox content and services, we expect revenue to decline in the low-teens, reflecting a prior year comparable that benefited from strong first-party content, as well as the recent price changes for Xbox Game Pass as we focus on delivering more value to gamers," she added. "Hardware revenue should decline year-over-year."

About the Author

Senior Editor, News, GameDeveloper.com

Game Developer news editor Chris Kerr is an award-winning reporter with over a decade of experience in the game industry. His byline has appeared in notable print and digital publications including Edge, Stuff, Wireframe, International Business Times, and PocketGamer.biz. Throughout his career, Chris has covered major industry events including GDC, PAX Australia, Gamescom, Paris Games Week, and Develop Brighton. 



https://www.gamedeveloper.com/business/xbox-delivers-record-monthly-active-users-but-hardware-revenue-is-still-in-decline

item.title: Asobo Teases Resonance: A Plague Tale Legacy Release Is ‘Almost Here’ After Nearly a Year of Silence
item.content:encoded:
It's been almost a year since Studio Asobo and Focus Home revealed Resonance: A Plague Tale Legacy, the third game in the peculiar action/adventure series.

After a long silence, the French studio has shared a lot of info about this prequel, including its development status, in a fresh devblog. There's good news on that front, as Resonance: A Plague Tale Legacy is in the final production phase with the last recording sessions and the final notes of the score wrapping up. The blog post teases that "the release is almost here", suggesting it will be released this year, possibly ahead of the behemoth called Grand Theft Auto VI (which is still scheduled for November 19). For the record, the previous game, A Plague Tale: Requiem, launched on October 18, 2022.

Resonance: A Plague Tale Legacy is set 15 years before the events seen in Requiem and takes place elsewhere, too: in the Mediterranean Sea, specifically on Minotaur's Island, a location said to conceal a secret treasure. The game shifts between two timelines via a mechanic called "Echoes": 14th-century medieval Greece and ancient Minoan civilization.

The protagonist, Sophia, is also partly new. She appeared as a companion in Requiem, but she'll be playable this time around, and she'll also spearhead the biggest change in the series: as a pirate smuggler, she has access to a full melee combat system with swords, daggers, and parries, whereas the previous protagonist, Amicia, used her sling as a ranged weapon.

The blog post dives into Sophia's different looks in this new game and also provides more context for her backstory. At a young age, she was placed in a convent by her mother's family, but escaped to her father, the leader of a pirate camp, where she was raised and trained to fight, eventually earning the nickname "The Scorpion of the Seas". The story shifts between her adult life and childhood flashbacks, exploring her motivations and the world she grew up in. Moreover, the Macula (the rat plague curse at the heart of the series) is directly linked to events on Minotaur's Island, and the devblog hints that Sophia herself may be affected by it, setting up deeply personal stakes for the story.

Resonance: A Plague Tale Legacy also features complex puzzles that will require using a stolen Minoan sphere to solve by manipulating light, and there's something restless lurking in the shadows of the island that follows Sophia around, increasing tension.

About the author: With over two decades of experience in gaming journalism, Alessio Palumbo has led the gaming vertical at Wccftech since August 2015. He started working at a young age for Italian websites like Everyeye.it, Gamestar.it, Nextgame.it, and Multiplayer.it before kickstarting the indie English-language publication Worlds Factory as its founder and Editor in Chief. In the last decade, he has coordinated the overall output of Wccftech's gaming section, managed PR relations, assigned reviews, produced daily news coverage, edited gaming content as needed, and delivered game reviews. Arguably, his trademark content is the long series of exclusive developer interviews that have been cited by Wikipedia and by the biggest news media and gaming publications. His passion for technology also makes him knowledgeable when it comes to gaming hardware and tech. His favorite genres include RPGs, MMORPGs, and action/adventure games.

item.title: Today’s Contexto: Hints & Answer For April 30, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Contexto is a word association game that gives you a new word to guess every single day. With every attempt, the AI algorithm determines how close your guess is to the daily answer based on thematic and contextual similarity.

Sometimes, though, no matter how experienced you are, the word you’re looking for can be incredibly vague and hard to deduce. Luckily for you, this guide is full of hints to give you a helping hand, but make sure you give today’s Contexto a go if you haven’t already!

How To Play Contexto

Contexto is incredibly simple to play, but difficult to master. All you have to do is type words into the search bar, and the AI algorithm will tell you how close your word is to the answer.

You’ll know how close your guess is based on the colour of the bar and the number next to it.

  • Red includes guesses with the number 1,501 and above, meaning you’re way off from the secret word.
  • Orange includes guesses between 301 and 1,500, meaning you’re getting closer, but still some ways off.
  • Green includes guesses between 1 and 300, meaning you’re on the right track.

Best Contexto Tips & Tricks

  • Start off using broad terms, then narrow down once you’ve got under the 1,000 mark.
  • Try using Nouns first, and dip into verbs or adjectives once you’ve discovered the context.
  • Experiment with random words at times, especially as different forms of a root word can rank differently, such as work and worker.
  • Guess away, as unlike other word games like Wordle, you aren’t limited to a strict number of attempts.

Best Starting Words For Contexto

Here are some great starting words to get you going with Contexto:

  • Thing
  • Person
  • Place
  • Animal
  • Work
  • Nature
  • Game
  • Concept
  • Idea
  • Food

Contexto Hints For Today, April 30, 2026

Without giving away today’s Contexto answer, here are a few helpful hints to get you thinking in the right direction:

  • Hint 1: Metal alloy
  • Hint 2: Made from copper and tin
  • Hint 3: Represents third place

If they don’t help, then here are the nine closest words, going from tenth to second:

  • Metal
  • Antique
  • Pewter
  • Plate
  • Nickel
  • Gold
  • Silver
  • Copper
  • Brass

Today’s Contexto Answer For April 30, 2026

If you’ve tried everything and still can’t figure out today’s Contexto answer, then you can find it down below:

Answer: Bronze

Bronze is a brown metal alloy made of copper and tin.

When Is The Next Contexto Game?

Contexto is updated daily at 12 AM or Midnight in your respective time zone, meaning the next game will start on May 1.

The Contexto schedule is very consistent, with a new game releasing at the same time every day.

How did you find today’s Contexto? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more word game guides, check out the following:

  • Wordle

Charlie is one of Insider Gaming's Guides Writers who's been in the gaming industry for a little over a year. Unfortunately for him, he enjoys every video game genre out…

item.title: Sony’s PlayStation DRM Mess Gets an Official Answer After Xbox One 2013 Comparisons Spread Across the Internet
item.content:encoded:
A Sony spokesperson has finally provided an official response to the PlayStation Online DRM controversy. Gamespot received the following statement after asking for some much-needed clarification:

Players can continue to access and play their purchased games as usual. A one-time online check is required to confirm the game's license, after which no further check-ins are required.

The controversy began in late April when several PlayStation users noticed a 30-day countdown timer appearing on the license information page of newly purchased digital games on PS4 and PS5. The timer displayed a "Valid Period" start and end date, along with "Remaining Time," sparking widespread fears that digital game licenses would expire if consoles weren't connected to the internet during that window.

The issue was first flagged publicly by YouTuber Modded Hardware, subsequently verified by DoesItPlay (a game preservation-focused account), and later shared widely by prominent modder Lance McDonald. The feature appears to affect only games purchased after the March 2026 system update, with older purchases remaining unaffected.

Initial responses from PlayStation's chat support made things worse, not better. Some support representatives confirmed the 30-day requirement was intentional and not a bug, while others told users no recurring check-in would be needed; these contradictory answers obviously amplified panic. PlayStation Support also confirmed that setting a console as "Primary" does not bypass the requirement.

The situation drew immediate comparisons to Microsoft's Xbox One DRM debacle in 2013, when the company infamously required an online check-in every 24 hours before backing down under massive public pressure.

Sony's statement to GameSpot appears to clarify that the timer is a one-time authentication mechanism, a temporary license that converts to permanent after the initial online check, rather than a recurring 30-day requirement. It has been suggested that the change may be aimed at combating fraud, potentially linked to refund scams or exploits.

However, Sony has not explained why the change was rolled out silently and without any public announcement, which remains the central criticism even after the clarifying statement. No doubt this could have been avoided with clearer and faster communication.

About the author: With over two decades of experience in gaming journalism, Alessio Palumbo has led the gaming vertical at Wccftech since August 2015. He started working at a young age for Italian websites like Everyeye.it, Gamestar.it, Nextgame.it, and Multiplayer.it before kickstarting the indie English-language publication Worlds Factory as its founder and Editor in Chief. In the last decade, he has coordinated the overall output of Wccftech's gaming section, managed PR relations, assigned reviews, produced daily news coverage, edited gaming content as needed, and delivered game reviews. Arguably, his trademark content is the long series of exclusive developer interviews that have been cited by Wikipedia and by the biggest news media and gaming publications. His passion for technology also makes him knowledgeable when it comes to gaming hardware and tech. His favorite genres include RPGs, MMORPGs, and action/adventure games.

item.title: Ideals Like Full-Time Employment and Job Security Are ‘Too Romantic’ in Game Dev, Says Monument Valley Studio CEO
item.content:encoded:
Ustwo CEO explains contractors will be used to lower development budgets.

The CEO of Monument Valley developer Ustwo Games, Maria Sayans, has outlined the studio needs to lower development costs, and to do so it will rely on contractors in the future rather than hiring full-time staff. Sayans explained the plan in an interview with Game Developer at London Games Fest.

In the wake of Netflix dropping the Monument Valley games from its service and deals with major companies drying up, Ustwo has a plan to focus on building “meaningful single player experiences” for PC and consoles. It’s a plan already in motion, with Ustwo recently porting several of its games to platforms like Steam and Switch without publisher support.

However, Sayans explained that they’ve subsequently learned that lower development budgets are going to be crucial.

“We saw a lot of potential for the Monument Valley IP to be maybe reset and reinvented for PC and consoles, but what became clear was that our development budgets were too high for us to achieve a safer break even if we were aiming for PC and console,” she said, explaining that the studio has been making games over three- to four-year production cycles for between £7 million to £10 million, and that they have to lower that cost – especially if and when working on new projects outside the established Monument Valley series.

“For example, if we did something like Alba or Assemble With Care, we would have to do that for a lot less money,” said Sayans. “There are people doing really, really well in those spaces on PC for much smaller budgets, that we will never be able to achieve because we’re based in London and have employees with pensions and so on.

“We’ve been a little bit too romantic about the idea that we should have employees and give people long-term job security. I think that got us into a place where, reaching the heights of Monument Valley 3 [production], contractors were always a relatively low percentage of our employee base.”

Sayans said Ustwo will be changing this balance in the future.

“I think going forward, we’ll see that we’ve got a core team and any growth will come through contractors, which is something I hate about the industry. I’ve been in the industry for 20 years, and those of us who joined in the early 2000s, we had it very good. You want to be able to give that kind of stability... but I think that's a shift in how we want to work with people going forward.”

Luke is a Senior Editor on the IGN reviews team. You can track him down on Bluesky @mrlukereilly to ask him things about stuff.

Recommends

In This Article



https://www.ign.com/articles/ideals-like-full-time-employment-and-job-security-are-too-romantic-in-game-dev-says-monument-valley-studio-ceo

item.title: When Will Fortnite Servers Be Back Up? Fortnite April 30 Downtime, Explained
item.content:encoded:
While Fortnite remains in the midst of Chapter 7, Season 2, a fresh update is arriving on April 30, meaning that the servers for the Epic Games title will be unavailable for a period of time.

Epic Games’ regular schedule of a new Fortnite update every two weeks has become a core part of the game, and following on from the last update on April 16, there’s fresh downtime taking place.

If you’re itching for your Fortnite fix and want to know when the servers will be available again, Insider Gaming has all the details.

When Will Fortnite Servers Be Back Up?

Fortnite servers are down for several hours for the April 30 update, although the exact time they will return has not been confirmed by Epic Games.

Downtime on April 30 is expected to begin at 4 AM ET, and while the exact length of time the servers will be unavailable before hasn’t been stated publicly, previous experience provides an expected time frame.

  • More Insider Gaming: Fortnite Offers Refunds for d4vd Items

Mid-season updates don’t have downtime as long as the seasonal transitions, like the move to a new Chapter. Instead, downtime lasts for around three to four hours on average.

As such, the Fortnite servers should be back up around 7 AM to 8 AM ET on April 30, though this is subject to change, particularly if any issues are discovered, and the best way to keep track of the server status is via social media platforms.

What’s in The Fortnite April 30 Update?

The Fortnite update on April 30 is considerably smaller than the previous two mid-season updates, particularly compared to the last update two weeks ago that debuted a new Fortnite Festival season and moved Save the World to a free-to-play model.

Instead, this update is focused largely on small gameplay changes, with fresh weapons in Battle Royale, a new Blitz Map, and a Reload Loot Pool Update.

  • More Insider Gaming: Can You Play Fortnite Save The World on Mobile?

As always, the Shop will be refreshed with new items, including the debut of freshly released cosmetic, and it’s a significant turning point, as it marks the first time that skins from the Absolute Doom Battle Pass from 2024.

However, it’s not clear yet what Fortnite’s plans are for returning skins from that Battle Pass once the exclusivity period ends, and they could be saved for a future drop.

What do you want to see Fortnite add in the next update? Share your thoughts in the Insider Gaming Discord.

For more Fortnite, find out if you can still get free V-Bucks in Save the World, and check out how to unlock the free Jess skin.

item.title: Kyoto Xanadu details more characters, Action Cards, Support Cards, and Aether
item.content:encoded:
Falcom has released new information and screenshots for action RPG Kyoto Xanadu introducing characters Utsuho Tatsumiya, Dogi, Suzume Sawatari, Kyoya Suo, Momiji Kisaragi, and Alusalu Minori Milvi, as well as how “Action Cards” and “Support Cards” are utilized in class, and offering Aether to the Sacred Tree.

Get the details below.

■ Characters

Utsuho Tatsumiya (voiced by Jun Fukushima)

  • Age: 35

An instructor at Hirasaka Academy and the homeroom teacher of Rei’s class. He teaches Japanese.

Fundamentally unmotivated, he makes no attempt to hide his languid demeanor, even in front of his students.

He often makes cynical and negative remarks, and at times even says things unbecoming of a teacher.

Dogi (voiced by Kenta Miyake)

  • Age: 34

A large, burly physical education instructor at Hirasaka Academy.

Despite his bold and carefree personality, he is cheerful and attentive, making him a reliable adult admired by students.

Although Dogi himself is not a Chosen, he possesses immense physical strength befitting his appearance—so much so that he can supposedly smash a solid wall to pieces with a single punch.

Suzume Sawatari (voiced by Rumi Okubo)

  • Age: 16
  • Soul Device: ???

A first-year high school student attending Hirasaka Academy as a Chosen.

She is shrewd and calculating, trading strategy data for Hirasaka Xanadu within the academy and independently profiling individuals that may require special caution.

Sensing a business opportunity in Rei, who declared on his first day as a transfer student that he would “conquer the entirety of Hirasaka Xanadu,” she approaches him in an attempt to sell her services.

Kyoya Suo (voiced by Kengo Kawanishi)

  • Age: 24
  • Soul Device: Pistol types

A young man who graduated from Hirasaka Academy and specializes in a tricky fighting style that combines martial arts with a pistol-type Soul Device.

He now works as a freelancer, accepting requests from all sorts of clients to deal with the other world and supernatural phenomena.

Easygoing and somewhat detached from the real world, he collects strange junk as countermeasures against other world encroachment—though this habit has partly turned into a hobby.

Momiji Kisaragi (voiced by You Taichi)

  • Age: 22

A staff member at Hirasaka Academy in charge of reception at the Xanadu Lobby.

Highly capable, she can handle any task with ease and provides thorough support to students taking on Xanadu.

Though not an instructor herself, she is highly trusted by the students, so much so that Rei has remarked she is “more of a teacher than Tatsumiya.”

Alusalu Minori Milvi (voiced by Rico Sasaki)

  • Age: 27

A half-Nordic woman who lives alone in Kyoto. She works as a model and is often seen strolling around the city in stylish outfits.

After Rei moves into the same apartment building, the two begin running into each other frequently as neighbors.

She is friendly and easygoing, and seems perfectly normal, but… from time to time, strange, hair-raising noises can be heard coming from her apartment next door.

■ System – Class: “Action Cards” and “Support Cards”

By taking classes at Hirasaka Academy, you can enhance your spiritual powers of “Wisdom,” “Virtue,” and “Valor,” which are used to strengthen your Soul Devices.

The basic class flow is as follows:

  1. On the “Class Preparation” screen, choose which “Action Cards” and “Support Cards” to use.
  2. Start the class.
  3. Earn Wisdom, Virtue, and Valor points based on the class results.

Action Cards

  • Action Cards can be used during the class preparation phase to enhance your focus, increasing the Wisdom, Virtue, and Valor points earned in class.
  • Focus points and Cost Points (CP) differ depending on the type of Action Card, and Action Cards can be used until your CP is depleted.
  • Cards with high Focus points also have high CP.

Support Cards

  • Support Cards can be used during the class preparation phase to gain beneficial effects.
  • Like Action Cards, some Support Cards require CP, so it is important to decide how to allocate your limited CP between Action Cards and Support Cards.
  • Additionally, once used, a Support Card cannot be reused until it recovers. However, it can be made available again by interacting with the corresponding character.

■ System – Facilities: “Sacred Tree”

By collecting otherworldly “Aether” into your “Aether Vial” during exploration of Xanadu and bringing it back to the real world to offer to the Sacred Tree on the academy grounds, you can receive various blessings.

Aether can be obtained by destroying terrain in Xanadu or by attacking and purifying stone statues corrupted by otherworldly miasma.

★★★ Warning: Collecting Too Much Aether ★★★

  • If you continue collecting Aether after your Aether Vial exceeds its capacity, there is a chance the vial will shatter, causing “Aether Poisoning.”
  • When afflicted with Aether Poisoning, your HP will decrease, Soul Accelerator cannot be used, and HP recovery and item use become unavailable. Symptoms worsen over time, making exploration of Xanadu increasingly difficult.

Kyoto Xanadu is due out for PlayStation 5, Switch 2, Switch, and PC via Steam on July 16 in Japan and Asia. A western release is also planned.

Watch a new set of character trailers below. View the screenshots at the gallery.

item.title: Utawarerumono: Past and Present Rediscovered details more characters, battle and town systems
item.content:encoded:
■ Characters

Amatsuki (voiced by Hitomi Ueda)

The captain of a ship of smugglers.

Her crewmen address her as “Mistress” as a sign of affection and respect.

Chicocoa (voiced by Akane Fujita)

A young woman from Arva Shulan.

She seems to have some connection to Shunya…

Shantuura (voiced by Kukuo Inoue)

The title given to the spiritual leader of Arva Shulan.

The current Shantuura is determined to do whatever it takes to give her people a real future.

Varga (voiced by Hiroki Maeda)

One of the Shantuura’s vassals.

A hot-blooded warrior who loves the thrill of battle and respects strength above all else.

He’s been sent on a mission to unearth some kind of ancient artefact.

Narakfala (voiced by Manabu Muraji)

One of the Shantuura’s vassals, mainly serving as her advisor.

With Mayacowl’s demise, he has also assumed the role of Wordsmith.

■ System

● Battle

Combat Skills and Okugi

Combat Skills are more powerful than normal attacks, but require MP to use. Each character has their own selection of skills to fulfill different roles in battle, including attacking, recovery, buffs and debuffs, and summoning. As the story progresses, you’ll get access to Okugi skills while in Overzeal. These consume even more MP than Combat Skills, but are powerful enough to turn the tide of a tough battle in your favor if used wisely.

Soulstones

Soulstones are used exclusively by Shunya. While in battle, she’ll recover one stone every turn. Soulstones are used to activate Shunya’s skills, many of which help power up allies or weaken enemies, making them useful when dealing with tough foes.

Keshin Transformation

Oshtor and Mikazuchi can now tap into the power of their akuruka to transform and unleash devastating new skills. While in Overzeal, use the “Assume Keshin Form” command to transform. The transformation will last for a limited number of turns. Rather than a set amount of MP, Keshin form skills consume a percentage of the total’s MP.

Examples:

  • Oshtor’s Keshin skill, Frozen Demise (AOE Water-Element Magecraft Attack) – Consumes 16% of max MP. Massive power. Inflicts Freeze on target. Greater Morale reduction against targets on smaller Action Rings.
  • Mikazuchi’s Keshin skill, Rakshasa (Non-Elemental Physical Attack) – Consumes 20% of max MP. Hits 2 times with strong-to-massive power. More powerful against targets with higher Morale.

● In Town

Requests

Check the Notice Board while in a town to see if anyone needs your help. You can view a request’s content and available rewards on the board, then speak to the client directly to accept it. Completing a request will earn you experience points as well as items, so consider giving them a try when you need that extra bit of experience to level up. You can also visit Yuuri in his room in Oshtor’s mansion for extra rewards after completing a certain number of requests. Think of them as a payment from the Imperial Court for keeping Yamato’s subjects safe and happy!

Investment and Fortification

Materials can be invested in the Imperial Capital’s Equipment Shop to expand its lineup. In this game, investment materials are treated as a different category than materials used to level up Halu. Invest enough in weapons, body armor, or leg armor to improve the quality of wares offered in that category. You can also use sen (money) to fortify your existing equipment. Fortifying will improve a piece of equipment’s stats, and may also give it additional effects and abilities.

● Other

Recollection Shrines

Mysterious shrines venerating the divine beast Nuko. They can be found throughout the land. Touch one to fully recover your HP and MP.

*Recollection Shrines appear in towns, the field, and dungeons.

Utawarerumono: Past and Present Rediscovered is due out for PlayStation 5 and Switch 2 in Japan and PC via Steam worldwide on May 28.



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/04/utawarerumono-past-and-present-rediscovered-details-more-characters-battle-and-town-systems

item.title: Animal Crossing: New Horizons 3.0.3 Patch Notes - Switch 2 And Switch Updates And Fixes
item.content:encoded:
Download it now

Animal Crossing: New Horizons received a special Animal Crossing anniversary update earlier this month, adding a leaf statue to the game.

Now, Nintendo has released Version 3.0.3, which makes this same item available for purchase from the Nook Shopping furniture catalog. It means even if you didn't already have it, you can now see it in there. There's also a price adjustment to this item, and to top it off are some bug fixes for the game on Switch 1 & 2.

If we hear anything else about this latest update for New Horizons, we'll let you know. Here's the full rundown via Nintendo's official support page:

Animal Crossing: New Horizons: Ver. 3.0.3 (Released April 29, 2026)

The following updates have been made when playing the game on Nintendo Switch 2 and Nintendo Switch:

General updates

  • The 25th Anniversary item leaf statue is now available for purchase from the Nook Shopping furniture catalog.
    • Even players who have not previously obtained the leaf statue will now see it in their catalog.
    • The price of the leaf statue has also been adjusted with this update.
  • Fixed an issue where images for some items would not display when opening your home storage or the hotel room decoration catalog.

In case you missed the previous update, the Animal Crossing series celebrated its 25th anniversary earlier this month. As part of the celebrations, it added the Animal Crossing GameCube soundtrack to Nintendo Music.

New Horizons also got a major free update earlier this year, adding a Resort Hotel, expanded storage, and much more. This was released alongside a Switch 2 Edition, which is available as a paid upgrade. You can find out more about all of this in our guide here on Nintendo Life.

Nintendo has also released a new update for Super Mario Galaxy 2 on the Switch today, adding a new story to the storybook.

Have you downloaded this update yet? Where did you put this leaf statue in the end? Let us know in the comments.

[source en-americas-support.nintendo.com]

Liam is a news writer and reviewer on Nintendo Life. He's been writing about games for more than 15 years and is a lifelong fan of many iconic video game characters.

item.title: Super Mario Galaxy 2 Updated To Version 1.4.0, Here Are The Full Patch Notes
item.content:encoded:
A new story has been added

Following a patch in February, Nintendo has today rolled out a new update for Super Mario Galaxy 2 on the Switch.

Surprisingly, this patch includes not just fixes but also a "new story" for the game's storybook. You can see how to access it in the official patch notes below.

Here's the full rundown of Version 1.4.0 via Nintendo's support page:

Super Mario Galaxy 2: Ver. 1.4.0 (Released April 29, 2026)

General

  • Several issues have been fixed and adjustments made to ensure a smoother gaming experience on both Nintendo Switch 2 and Nintendo Switch.
  • A new story has been added to the storybook. Once Final Chapter is available in the storybook, you can read the new story by clearing any galaxy and earning a Power Star.

Note: The software must be updated to Ver. 1.2.0 or later to play on Nintendo Switch 2.

As reported by Vooks.net, the lighting in "Tall Trunk Galaxy" has also apparently been fixed in this latest game update. The same site has shared a video of the new storybook epilogue featuring Rosalina.

Apart from this, the Nintendo Music app has today added a new song from the latest Super Mario Galaxy 2 storybook chapter.

According to Nintendo's support page, the first Super Mario Galaxy game for Switch is still running on Version 1.3.1, which was released in February 2026.

Have you downloaded this update yet? Notice anything else? Let us know in the comments.

[source en-americas-support.nintendo.com]

item.title: Tony Hawk Once Modded His Friend’s PS1 to Get Early THPS Feedback
item.content:encoded:
"I hope I don't get in trouble for this."

Tony Hawk once modded his friend’s PlayStation 1 in order to get feedback on early builds of the original Tony Hawk’s Pro Skater. This is according to the Birdman himself, who told the story during a panel at iicon (a new conference for video game industry professionals) about the lasting legacy of the THPS series.

Back in 1998, Hawk was working with Neversoft on early versions of the soon-to-be legendary skateboarding game, which at the time was borne out of a modified version of the failed Bruce Willis-branded game Apocalypse.

“Every week they would burn a CD for me, and I played it on my modified PlayStation 1,” Hawk said. “I would make comments, and then the next week I get a disc in the mail with all the updates.”

“I was really excited about it, but I was also kind of isolated in my excitement because I was the only real skater working on it, and so – I hope I don’t get in trouble for this, 20 plus years later – but I started making copies of the burns and sending them to skaters that I knew were hardcore gamers.”

Hawk said at one point he modified his friend Atiba’s PS1 – presumably referring to skater and photographer Atiba Jefferson, who later appeared in THPS 4 – to be able to play the burned game discs in order to give him feedback.

“He really did have valuable feedback, and he started letting people come over to play the game. I mean, we were going rogue,” Hawk said.

But at one point, Hawk started hearing rumors within the hardcore skate community, with comments like “have you guys played ‘the game?'” starting to circulate.

“That's when I knew we did something right, and that the skateboarders were going to enjoy this," Hawk said. “And to me, that was the mark of success. I had no idea that it would ever transcend just the skateboard world, but the fact that they just referred to it as ‘the game’ gave me a sense of pride.”

"The fact that they just referred to it as ‘the game’ gave me a sense of pride.”“

Elsewhere in the panel, Hawk commented that, if they get to make another one, Tony Hawk’s Underground is the next game he’d like to see remastered.

“What’s next, that’s always the conversation. Because we are now making our way through the series as remasters and remakes, and to be honest, the most noise that I hear about remastering the game, is for Underground, and has been for the last few years. I don’t know who I’m pitching this to, but that’s the one, if we get to do it.”

However, the developerment team behind the recent THPS 3+4 remaster, Iron Galaxy, faced another round of layoffs earlier this month which follows a significant round of layoffs from 2025.

For more from iicon, check out our writeup of Take Two CEO Strauss Zelnick’s comments about the much-debated price point of GTA 6.

Bo Moore is IGN's Senior Manager of Tech. You can find him online @usebomswisely.

Recommends

In This Article



https://www.ign.com/articles/tony-hawk-once-modded-his-friends-ps1-to-get-early-thps-feedback

item.title: Amid Era Of Unprecedented Authoritarian Overreach, New Bipartisan House Resolution Condemns Hasan Piker For Some Reason
item.content:encoded:
Some politicians continue to think that the greatest threat facing America today is a Twitch streamer

Published: April 29, 2026, 9:41 pm

Last updated: April 29, 2026, 9:52 pm

In the latest escalation of the nationwide brain plague that is pundits and politicians’ complete inability to be normal about Hasan Piker, two members of the US House of Representatives, Democrat Josh Gottheimer and Republican Mike Lawler, introduced a bipartisan resolution “condemning the rise of antisemitic, hate-filled rhetoric disseminated by prominent online personalities, including Hasan Piker and Candace Owens, and calling on social media platforms and public leaders to take stronger action against hate.” This doesn’t really do anything, but it sure sends a message about where their priorities lie.

The resolution does its best to decontextualize and equivocate the statements of Piker and Owens, two online personalities with radically opposed beliefs, by cherry picking a greatest hits collection of supposed “gotcha” moments from over the years. For example:

Whereas Hasan Piker, a prominent online streamer, has often used antisemitic rhetoric, including expressing support for Hamas, a designated foreign terrorist organization;
Whereas, in August 2019, during a stream on Twitch, Hasan Piker stated that ‘‘America deserved 9/11’’;
Whereas Hasan Piker referred to Orthodox Jews as ‘‘inbred’’;
Whereas Hasan Piker downplayed sexual violence committed by Hamas, a designated foreign terrorist organization, against innocent civilians on October 7, 2023, stating, ‘‘It doesn’t matter if f-ing rapes happened on October 7th, like that doesn’t change the dynamic for me’’;
Whereas Hasan Piker has expressed support for Hamas on multiple occasions, including on April 14, 2026, stating, ‘‘Hamas over Israel every single time’’ and doubling down on claims in multiple media appearances that Hamas is ‘‘a thousand times better than Israel’’;
Whereas Candace Owens has employed rhetoric that has included conspiracy theories accusing Israel of controlling the United States Government, promoting false claims that Jews are taught by ancient religious texts to hate non-Jews, and casting doubt on the truth of the stories of Holocaust survivors;
Whereas, on February 1, 2026, Candace Owens posted on X an antisemitic claim that the United States is controlled by ‘‘satanic pedophiles who work for Israel’’;
Whereas, during a podcast appearance on December 8, 2025, Owens repeated false claims that Jews are killing Christian children as part of religious rituals and that Jewish texts and traditions promote the sexual assault of children, which is an antisemitic trope constituting a blood libel;
Whereas, in July 2024, Candace Owens called the horrific medical experiments committed by Josef Mengele on Jews during the Holocaust ‘‘bizarre propaganda’’, continuing that ‘‘some of the stories, by the way, sound completely absurd’’, downplaying and rejecting the veracity of these crimes;
Whereas, on February 21, 2025, Candace Owens hosted E. Michael Jones, who claimed on her platform that Hollywood ‘‘created the Holocaust narrative’’;

The most crucial issue here is that Owens is an actual antisemitic conspiracy theorist—something you can easily discern based on the timbre of her comments even in the resolution—while Piker merely opposes Israel. The latter is a common sentiment among those on the far left and also in America more generally, especially following October 7 and Israel’s brutal genocide of Palestinians. 

The US government’s right and (right-leaning) left, however, consider Israel a close ally and have contributed hundreds of billions of dollars to the West’s favorite destabilizing force in the Middle East, making it “the largest cumulative recipient of US foreign assistance since World War II.” With social media opening people’s eyes more widely than ever to the atrocities continued by Israel—and a wildly unpopular war in Iran still fresh on everyone’s minds—Republicans and Democrats have united to lug out the only weapon left in their arsenal: naked suppression of speech. 

This after Democrats were aghast at Jimmy Kimmel’s temporary removal from ABC in September 2025 following a joke about Charlie Kirk, a removal that FCC chairman Brendan Carr openly encouraged and applauded. During a Senate Commerce Committee hearing in December, Democrats spent hours taking Carr to task for trampling the First Amendment. Republicans have now redoubled their targeting of ABC and efforts to get Kimmel fired following a joke about Trump’s health during a pre-White House Correspondents’ Association Dinner segment.

Despite ostensibly being in favor of the First Amendment, prominent Democrats have repeatedly called for Piker to be punished and even outright censored for speech that criticizes both major US political parties and, of course, Israel. Now Gottheimer joins that chorus. 

“Hatred is hatred, period. It doesn’t matter whether it comes from the far right or the far left,” he said today when introducing the resolution. “We cannot be selective in calling out antisemitism. When influential voices spread conspiracy theories, promote terrorism, or dehumanize Jewish people, it fuels real-world violence and intimidation. We must stand up and speak out.”

It is doubly difficult to view Gottheimer and Lawler’s resolution as anything more than laughably disingenuous considering that it was proposed on the same day as numerous events that pose an actual threat to Americans of all stripes, such as the Supreme Court’s latest attack on the Voting Rights Act—a nakedly partisan effort to permanently consolidate power in the hands of the right. The Twitch streamer is definitely the real problem, though.

For his part, Piker believes he knows what all of the furor around him from both Democrats and Republicans is actually about.

"I am the danger. We are the danger. I need them to understand this: We're dangerous to the corporate bottom line. We're dangerous to profit seekers,” Piker said during an interview with Joy Reid today. “That's the only thing that our politicians and many in the media care about right now, so in some respects [Republicans who call my rhetoric 'dangerous'] are correct. … I don't mean divine retribution or punishment of any sort; I mean the worst punishment of all, where they can no longer justify multi-million dollar salaries. I am a danger to those who seek profit and put the interests of profit over the interests of people. And that's precisely the reason why they attack people like myself. Because I'm beholden to no one but my own community. I'm beholden to no one but the American working class, which as Zohran [Mamdani] also says, is the majority in this country."

Recommended

Nathan Grayson

Co-owner of the good website Aftermath. Reporter interested in labor and livestreaming. Send tips to nathan@aftermath.site or nathangrayson.666 on Signal.

All articles



https://aftermath.site/hasan-piker-bipartisan-house-resolution-candace-owens/

item.title: Tales Of Eternia Remastered Switch Rating Surfaces Online
item.content:encoded:
Bandai Namco isn't slowing down

It looks like the PEGI ratings board may have revealed the return of Tales of Eternia ahead of schedule.

A new rating has temporarily appeared on the classification website for "Tales of Eternia Remastered" on the Nintendo Switch.

The publisher is listed as Bandai Namco Entertainment Europe, and attached to the listing is a 'PEGI 12' rating along with a brief outline of the action role-playing title:

"Role-playing game which follows the story of Reid and his friends, Farah and Keele, as they meet a mysterious girl named Meredy who speaks an unknown language. Their subsequent quest to discover her origins leads them across a dimensional boundary to an entirely different realm known as Celestia."

Tales of Eternia made its debut on Sony's PlayStation in the year 2000 and is part of Bandai Namco's 'Tales of' series. Tales of Berseria Remastered was released on the Switch earlier this year in February.

If we get an official update or announcement about this Tales of Eternia Remastered, we'll let you know.

Have you played this game? Would you be interested in a remastered release? Let us know in the comments.

[source pegi.info, via gematsu.com]

item.title: SUNSOFT announces car chase action revival ROUTE-16R for Switch, PC alongside classic re-releases
item.content:encoded:
• PC
  • PS5
  • Switch

Due out on August 6.

Publisher SUNSOFT and developer Wit One have announced ROUTE-16R, a new entry in the classic ROUET-16 series originally released in the 1980s. It will launch for Switch and PC via Steam on August 6 for 3,300 yen.

Additionally, the following two classic ROUTE-16 titles will also be available:

  • ROUTE-16 (Switch) – 814 yen
  • ROUTE-16 TURBO (PS5, Switch, PC [Steam]) – 1,540 yen

In Japan, a ROUTE-16 COLLECTION will also be for Switch. It will cost 5,478 yen for the physical edition and 4,730 yen for the digital edition. It includes ROUTE-16R, ROUTE-16</em., and ROUTE-16 TURBO.

Here is an overview of ROUTE-16R, via its Steam page:

About

ROUTE-16R is a brand-new entry in the classic ROUTE-16 series, originally released in the 1980s.

Avoid enemy vehicles and monsters as you race through the stage, collecting every item scattered across the maze.

Switch dynamically between Maze Mode, where you navigate intricate labyrinths filled with traps and gimmicks, and Radar Mode, which reveals the entire stage made up of 16 mazes-delivering a high-speed chase experience that surpasses the original games.

Key Features

  • Don’t Just Run—Fight Back with the Evolved MAD X-R
    • Unlike previous ROUTE-16 titles, colliding with enemies triggers a transformation: MAD X-R shifts into a combat robot, initiating battle.
    • Use your upgraded MAD X-R’s attacks along with SP items collected throughout the stage to deal damage and defeat your enemies.
  • Customization
    • Before entering a stage, use the money you’ve recovered to customize your MAD X-R.
    • Enhance core attributes like fuel and speed, as well as combat performance, to suit your playstyle.
  • Boss Battles
    • The Kanharoo Gang won’t go down without a fight.
    • Collecting all items won’t end the stage-doing so will trigger a boss encounter.
    • Bosses unleash intense attacks to corner MAD X-R.
    • Dodge their assaults, find openings in their defenses, and strike back by transforming into robot form to deal decisive damage!
  • Multiplayer
    • Multiplayer mode is included for the first time in the series.
    • Compete in a mini-game where players battle to capture the flag.
    • Enjoy fast-paced, arcade-style chase action with friends and family!
    • (Local multiplayer only. Online multiplayer is not supported.)

Watch the announcement trailer and first gameplay below. Visit the official website here.

Announce Trailer

Title Screen

Announce Gameplay

item.title: Dragon Quest XI S: Echoes Of An Elusive Age - Definitive Edition Has Been Rated For Switch 2
item.content:encoded:
Will the hero return?

Ahead of Dragon Quest Day next month, it looks like another entry in the series could be on the way to the Switch 2.

A new classification for a Switch 2 version of Dragon Quest XI S: Echoes of an Elusive Age - Definitive Edition has popped up on the classification board in Taiwan.

This 'S' version of the title made its debut on the original Nintendo Switch in 2019 and is described as the ultimate edition of the excellent RPG, adding additional character-specific stories, a new orchestral soundtrack and much more.

We awarded this Square Enix game nine out of ten stars, calling it "the gift that keeps on giving":

"Dragon Quest XI S: Echoes of an Elusive Age - Definitive Edition is an achievement that Square should be proud of; this is one of the best games they’ve put out in years. A heartwarming, well-paced narrative supported by a cast of fantastic characters, a dense and interesting overworld packed with dozens of hours of content, and one of the finest soundtracks we’ve heard in a JRPG combine to make this an unforgettable modern classic. Whether you’re a newcomer to the JRPG genre or a returning vet, do yourself a favor and buy Dragon Quest XI S: Echoes of an Elusive Age as soon as you’re able."

Of course, this is just a rating, so before we get too excited about a Switch 2 version, keep in mind nothing has been officially announced, and a rating doesn't always guarantee a release.

Would you be interested in revisiting this title on the Switch 2? Have you already played through it on the Switch? Let us know in the comments.

[source gamerating.org.tw]

Liam is a news writer and reviewer on Nintendo Life. He's been writing about games for more than 15 years and is a lifelong fan of many iconic video game characters.

item.title: Cel-shaded co-op first-person shooter All Eyes Above announced for PC
item.content:encoded:
• PC

Fight a ruthless faction in a moving battlefield.

Ultimate Studio has announced All Eyes Above, a cel-shaded cooperative first-person shooter for up to four players with a 10-mission campaign. It will be available for PC via Steam. A release date was not announced.

Here is an overview of the game, via its Steam page:

About

All Eyes Above is a cooperative shooter for a crew of up to four players, where your teamwork is the only thing standing between civilization and obliteration. Join the ranks of elite soldiers and fight a ruthless faction in a moving battlefield across an epic 10 mission campaign.

Move. Hide. Fight.

In All Eyes Above, you and your crew are the last line of defense between civilization and complete annihilation. An all-powerful satellite network has destroyed life as we know it and will not stop until it eradicates anything that moves. While the inevitable destruction continues, you battle a ruthless faction that seeks total control of the remaining resources.

Become a Rider

As the Archangel satellite system destroys life on Earth, some cities remain alive and in hiding. Heaven17 is your oasis in the wasteland, but it relies on a small group of elite soldiers to keep it running. With resources at a critical level, you and your friends are conscripted to save the day. As a rider you must fight in a high-velocity resource war where each mission could be your last.

A Battlefield of Moving Vehicles

The Archangel rewrites the rules of war. In order to avoid a weapon that is constantly searching for them, riders must protect the one thing that keeps them hidden while fighting in moving battlefields. With the Oppressors striving for control, your crew will have to defend your ship and destroy your adversaries. Use grapples, harpoons, high-tech weapons and even exploit the Archangel network itself to strike back. But, don’t forget about what’s happening on your own ship; repair and control it to stay in the fight.

Are You a Hero of Heaven17

In this 10 mission campaign, your crew will grapple around ships, hack aboard enemy transports, and capture Angelite fuel while fending off hordes of Oppressors and avoiding detection. Heaven17 has kept your crew alive throughout everything and now it’s up to you to protect them! Are you ready?

Watch the announcement trailer below.

Announce Trailer



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/04/cel-shaded-co-op-first-person-shooter-all-eyes-above-announced-for-pc

item.title: Wizard101 Is Running Two Free Events Right Now For Its Dungeons And Castles
item.content:encoded:
The free all-ages MMORPG Wizard101 is running two free events for its players: one for dungeons, and one to promote visiting castles and houses around the game.  

The current combat event is a Portal Peril series, where once a week every month, players can tackle a past dungeon, known in Wizard101 as a Housing Gauntlet, for free—and this time, it includes a higher-tier battle as well. 

Now until May 4, players can take on the Great Clock Gauntlet, playable at three tiers at level 50, 100, and a new 150 tier, the latter of which features “variations on the traditional monsters.” Players will be scaled up to the highest level in the party; all players can try twice per day for classic rewards as well as Scroll of Fortune progress. 

The other ongoing Wizard101 event right now until May 4 is the Spiral Scene event, where players can progress by rating castle tour houses, visiting top houses and completing daily assignments, plus other tasks. The free listed rewards for this are a Professor’s Hoard Pack, Mega Snacks, Jewels, and Reagents; plus, these tasks count towards the Scroll of Fortune. 

Nearly eighteen years after its launch, Wizard101 is not only chugging along, but seemingly doing its best to thrive in the MMORPG market, with over a million downloads across all of its recent console releases. 



https://www.mmorpg.com/news/wizard101-is-running-two-free-events-right-now-for-its-dungeons-and-castles-2000137937

item.title: Sony Responds to PlayStation DRM Fears With Official Statement
item.content:encoded:
PlayStation has clarified concerns about DRM (Digital Rights Management) raised by players regarding their digital purchases.

In late April 2026, PlayStation players took to social media to express their worries about a new timer linked to their digital games. This timer required them to check in with an active internet connection every 30 days to maintain the license for their purchases.

Initially, there was confusion about the legitimacy of this new DRM system, with rumors suggesting it might be an unintended bug related to an update. In response, Sony has now issued an official statement on the matter.

Sony Addresses PlayStation DRM Timer Concerns From Players

Sony Interactive Entertainment has issued an official statement to GameSpot clarifying the matter. According to a representative, players will be able to access their games as usual. However, a single-time check-in will be required, which requires an active internet connection.

“Players can continue to access and play their purchased games as usual. A one-time online check is required to confirm the game’s license, after which no further check-ins are required.”

The concern for most players is similar to that which is raised around Nintendo Switch 2 Game Key Cards: The need for an internet connection to access your purchased content. Yes, it is 2026, and most people have multiple ways to get online, but some raise concerns about that being a requirement and about the inability to play purchased titles in the future without modern-day internet access.

There may be some who take solace in the fact that there’s no need for a monthly check-in, but for now, it seems this is where things stand regarding PlayStation digital purchases. Insider Gaming will continue to monitor the story and report any updates as more information becomes available.

Sound off about this story and all others in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android

item.title: The Alchemist of Ars Magna coming to PS5, PS4 on September 17
item.content:encoded:
• PS4
  • PS5

Currently available for Switch and PC.

Publisher COSEN and developer Ninetail will release PlayStation 5 and PlayStation 4 versions of dungeon RPG The Alchemist of Ars Magna on September 17, the companies announced. It will support English and Japanese voice-overs, and English, German, Spanish, Japanese, Korean, Traditional Chinese, and Simplified Chinese text.

The Alchemist of Ars Magna first launched for PC via Steam on March 31, 2023, followed by Switch on November 7, 2024.

In Japan, the PlayStation 5 version will be available both physically and digitally. The physical standard edition is priced at 7,700 yen, while the limited edition is priced at 12,100 yen. The latter includes the original soundtrack, an acrylic stand, a pinup card, and a digital art book.

Here is an overview of the game, via COSEN:

About

The main character enters a school and gains experience in research, study, and love. This is the adventure story of an alchemist who grows through encounters and experiences and paves the way.

Story

Once upon a time, the founder of alchemy led people from a dying world to a new land. In this fertile and harmonious land where man-made “Gods of Creation” act as guardians, humans lead a peaceful existence.

However, a long time has since passed, and the “Dust” of death began to engulf Arcaus. Gods gone mad become heinous “Gods of Misfortune” and begin to devour the world, and ash demons born of dust bare its fangs at the living. Like burnt pieces of paper and worm-eaten clothes, the world finds itself in an era where the earth was being eroded by the ashes.

The people were not entirely unprepared, however. Alchemists who wished to carry on the miracles brought upon by its founder gather at the Cagliostro Academy, a school boasting the highest levels of wisdom.

Among them is another—a young alchemist who knocks on the doors of the academy with aspirations in mind.

Key Features

  • An RPG Set in an Academy for Alchemists – Oblivion is a natural disaster which is set to destroy the world. The protagonist enters the Cagliostro Academy to learn and master the art of combat. And in a school where many alchemists gather, the protagonist of the story accumulates experience through research, study, and love. This is a story of an alchemist who carves out his own path, growing through his encounters and experiences.
  • The World’s Guardians, “Gods”, and the “Dust” That Threaten Them – The world is controlled by a number of man-made gods. However, the “Dust,” an entity that calls for destruction, not only turns everything in its path into dust, but also contaminates the gods, turning them heinous. With this, alchemists are forced to deal with former gods that have now been turned into guiding forces of misfortune.
  • Make a Pact With the Great Origin and Save the World! – The Origin are five gods of varying attributes who serve as the pinnacle of all gods.
  • Equip This and Become Stronger—Crests Are a Testament of Your Contract With the Gods – You can obtain a crest, or crystals that contain the power of the gods by making a contract with them. Crests can be equipped to personal weapons known as ether gear. This gives the alchemists additional blessings of various skills. As you explore Arcaus, you can build trusting relationships with various gods to help your allies get stronger.
  • The Philosopher’s Stone and Corruption From the Dust Will Determine Your Fate – With the Pholosopher’s Stone in his body, the protagonist is able to convert dust and ash into energy. However, the more he uses his powers, the more contamination accumulates his body. And if left unchecked, the contamination will eventually become a disaster that could change the fate of the protagonist.

Band Together Against the Dust

Fend off demons of evil that are trying to turn the world into one of nothing but emptiness and dust!

  • Ash Demon – A demon that was reborn as a monster after having been decomposed by the Dust.
  • Heinous God – A being that was a guardian of the world, but has been contaminated by the Dust.
  • Skill-Based Turn Battles – Battles are turn-based, with a maximum of a party of five battling another party of five. You must use individual skills of each character wisely to win the battles.

Contract with the Gods

Proceed through the story and overcome the trials to obtain the crests, a proof that you have made your contract with the gods!

  • Crest – A proof of the contract between you and the gods, the world’s guardians. Their powers reside in crystals known as crests.
  • Gods With Unique Characteristics and Personalities – The gods you make contract with will vary in appearance and ability. You will be able to make contracts with many different gods as you travel around the continent.
  • Quest Field – Quests take you to a field comprised of a turn-based movement system. Depending on the points, you may run into monsters and/or be able to obtain items.

Equipping Crests

Make sure to arm your character with crests to draw upon the blessings of the gods!

  • Blessings of the Gods – Armor unique to alchemists, known as ether gear, can be equipped with crests from the gods. Status increases to the character and inherited unique abilities will depend on the characteristics of each god. Blessings from the gods will also change value attributes that have an effect in combat.
  • Ability – Something that is granted by a crest, or a part of a character’s individual features. This is what is automatically activated at the start of a battle.

Synthesizing

Work together with your heroine to create items from alchemy!

  • Synthesis System – Alchemy items that can support you in battle and while you are exploring. Alchemy items can be used to create quest items required to progress in the storyline.
  • Equipment Enhancement – Ether gears are personal equipment that can be enhanced in different stages. The more you strengthen your gear, the more the powerful skills will be unlocked.
  • Skills – The skills that you choose to perform during battle is unique to your character. These skills, including skills for attack, support, recovery, and more, are essential for achieving victory.

School Life

Get to know fellow heroine who will walk beside you in the alchemy academy. There will even be times where you get to compete with rival schools!

  • Harmonia School – A school of alchemists who advocate harmony between the world and its gods. The protagonist also belongs to this school. This school values the ancient principles of the founding alchemist.
  • Mardock School – A radical school that actively seeks to destroy heinous gods and try to use them in their research. This school tends to clash with Harmonia School because of the way it treats gods and heinous gods.

Watch a new trailer below.

PlayStation 5 and PlayStation 4 Announce Trailer



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/04/the-alchemist-of-ars-magna-coming-to-ps5-ps4-on-september-17

item.title: Bleeding Board Game Giant CMON Thinks, In The Year Of Our Lord 2026, That Investing In NFT Games Is The Future
item.content:encoded:
'By participating in Web3 projects, the Company can demonstrate its commitment to social responsibility and sustainability'

Published: April 29, 2026, 7:25 pm

CMON, long one of the biggest names in board games publishing, has had a very rough few years, losing tens of millions of dollars as it struggles to adapt to a market caught right in the middle of massive shifts in the global economic and political landscape. The good news is that the company has a plan to get itself out of this mess. The bad news is that it's maybe the worst plan imaginable.

Despite being well into the red, CMON's board have just decided to spend over $2 million buying Blissful Link, a company that makes play-to-earn NFT games, and use that foothold as a means to turn some of their traditional board games into "high-quality digital assets". By which they mean "video games with NFTs in them".

Here's an excerpt from the company's purchase filing that is, in 2026 and with the NFT boom already in the history books, staggering reading:

Digital transitioning would have the benefits of enhanced visual effects, have apps that handle scoring, timing etc, would enable a diversified number of players and are more accommodating to players not within the same vicinity. Added to this, entering into Web3 projects often emphasise social responsibility and ethical practices such as transparency and fairness on decision marking. By participating in Web3 projects, the Company can demonstrate its commitment to social responsibility and sustainability. However, the Group would continue to supplement this digital transformation as physical games would still offer a “screen break” for individuals as well as foster direct face to face interaction.

Social responsibility! Screen breaks! Web3 bullshit in the year 2026! None of this works or is real! Whoever thought of this, let alone signed off on it, isn't fit to get my kid out of the $10 boba debt he's in, let alone a company owing tens of millions of dollars.



https://aftermath.site/cmon-nft-web3-board-games/

item.title: DC Universe Online Brings Back Recruitment Event With Rewards For New Characters
item.content:encoded:
After passing over the mountain of running for 15 years, DC Universe Online has just brought back its Ultimate Recruitment Event, giving players new and old a little boost for entry-level content. 

Players who complete the tutorial with a character made during this event will be able to claim a Gotham City Emergency Signal. This nifty action provides a 25% experience boost (though provides no other stats), and it enables players to call a “strong Gotham ally;” until level 25, it’ll auto-defeat non-boss characters, and it gives a strong area-wide damage above that level. 

Additionally, players can get three more rewards by completing the event: an Umbral Green Aura effect, a Rank 120 Sinestro's Corrupted Power Ring, 100 Mogo Sprout Supply Boxes, and 10 Rage Battery Remnants for boosting Maverick Jackets or Boots. 

This is only for newly-made characters, and more specifically, it’s once per account—so per the event’s purpose, it’s a good excuse to rope some friends into the 15-year old MMORPG. On the flip side, all players will get an extra free character slot, so older players don’t have to worry about getting locked out of joining friends that way.

Plus, the Patrol 2 for this year’s Campaign has been delayed to May 5; the UI for the current Campaign should have been changed, at least, through yesterday’s maintenance. More details on this and the event are on the announcement blog. 



https://www.mmorpg.com/news/dc-universe-online-brings-back-recruitment-event-with-rewards-for-new-characters-2000137939

item.title: Aion's Dread Blade Update Brings a True Hybrid Class, Four New Dungeons, Enhancement Changes, and More
item.content:encoded:
The new Dread Blade update for Aion is out now, centered around the Dread Blade class, the MMORPG’s first true hybrid class. The update also adds new dungeons, revamped enchantments, and events to help you jump in with the new class.

The Dread Blade uses both weapons and magic. Their kit features abilities that can inflict damage and status effects on enemies, including multiple enemies, as well as cure their own debuffs and even restore their status.They are equipped with a range of AoE skills to control a battlefield and sometimes, with something like Final Execution, move quickly to low-health targets for a quick elimination. The control that this class has and those methods of taking out priority targets, can help them be a valuable member of a party.

To go with the new class, there’s a Dread Blade update gift to claim, along with the Dread Blade Starter Coupon, to get a bunch of helpful stuff to get you started on your new Dread Blade.

There are four new dungeons in the update: Tiamat’s Mind, Immortal Trial, Infinite Cube, and Boundary of Light and Darkness. Boundary of Light and Darkness is a starter dungeon that is appropriate for new players to take on some challenges and earn some helpful loot. Immortal Trial is a daily solo dungeon you can jump into for rewards. Infinite Cube features a dynamic, changing encounter. To succeed, every team member has to master their assigned role. Tiamat’s Mind features four floors to conquer. 

The update also makes a few changes to enchantments in an overhaul. Two major points are the removal of downgrades so that enchantment level is maintained even if you fail, and the introduction of fail-safe enchant. The latter means you will have a 100% success rate for all manastones. 

The update is out now on Nexon’s Purple platform on both Classic and Ascend servers. 



https://www.mmorpg.com/news/aions-dread-blade-update-brings-a-true-hybrid-class-four-new-dungeons-enhancement-changes-and-more-2000137940

item.title: Modiphius Announces Star Trek Adventures Allies and Adversaries Sourcebook, Adding New Alien Aid and Threats
item.content:encoded:
A new Star Trek Adventures sourcebook is now available. Modiphius Entertainment's new book, Star Trek Adventures Allies and Adversaries, provides rules for many beloved alien organizations from the iconic sci-fi series, greatly expanding potential stories at the gaming table.

 According to an official press release, Star Trek Adventures Allies and Adversaries is a new supplement for Star Trek Adventures Second Edition. The material is compatible both with the core rules and the solo adventure rules found in Star Trek Captains Log. 

The book will allow players and GMs to learn more about the many alien factions in the universe of Star Trek. They can create, modify, and use interesting, and cunning allies and adversaries, bringing them to life in dramatic, engaging stories within single missions or even an entire campaign.

This 128-page supplement will include the following:

  • Guidance on using NPCs as helpful allies, expanding on material found in the Core Rulebook
  • Information on several factions, including the Breen Confederacy, the Cardassian Union, the Dominion, the Gorn Hegemony, the Orion Syndicate, the Klingon Empire, the Romulan Star Empire, the Tholians, the Borg Collective, and many more
  • Game stats for dozens of Minor, Notable, and Major NPCs, among the various factions mentioned above
  • Game stats for several Star Trek creatures as well as rules and guidance on how to create your own original creatures

Star Trek Adventures Allies and Adversaries continues Modiphius' considerable long-term support for the officially licensed TTRPG. It joins the Species Sourcebook, which provided rules on playing as different iconic races, a tie-in book based on the animated show, Lower Decks, and prewritten campaigns like The Federation-Klingon War. Given the series'  sixty-year history, there is plenty to pull from.

Star Trek Adventures Allies and Adversaries is available for pre-order now from Modiphius' webstore at an MSRP of $55. The books are expected to ship on July 2026.

Ever since he was small, Tyler Chancey has had a deep, abiding love for video games and a tendency to think and overanalyze everything he enjoyed. This… More about Tyler

  • Announcement

  • RPG
